feed.title: electricfox.xyz
feed.subtitle: a simple blog for Linux and tech related things, also weekly pictures of foxes.

entry.title: What’s the best linux distro for beginners?

link.title: What’s the best linux distro for beginners?

entry.content:
Welcome traveller!

Are you new to the world of Linux and unsure about which distribution (distro) to choose? With so many options available, it can be quite overwhelming. In this blog post I’ll show you some of the best distros for beginners that cater to various needs and preferences. Either one of these distros I list will leave you well off.

Linux Mint

Linux mint is one of the most popular Linux distributions among new beginners due to its Windows 10 like interface, and extensive support. It offers a variety of preinstalled applications that cater to various needs such as office productivity, multimedia playback, web browsing, and more. The Linux Mint community provides excellent support through Reddit, forms, and Discord.

Fedora

Fedora is a distro sponsored by Red Hat that focuses on providing the latest technologies and software updates to its users, while being stable at the same time. It’s a little more challenging to use than Linux Mint but offers the best preformance for gaming, programming, and other workstation tasks. It also has excellent support.

So in conclusion, If you’re looking for a user-friendly interface similar to Windows 10 and require standard web browsing capabilities, Linux Mint is perfect for you. On the other hand, if you are a gamer or need to perform professional tasks, Fedora offers cutting-edge software updates and more. Additionally, Fedora is great if you have a newer laptop and are more comfortable with computers. No matter which distro you choose, remember that the Linux community is always ready to help and support new users in their journey.

TLDR;

Linux Mint - A user-friendly interface similar to Windows 10 with extensive documentation and preinstalled applications for office productivity, multimedia playback, web browsing, etc.

Fedora - Focuses on providing the latest technologies and software updates, great for gaming, programming, newer harderware, workstation tasks, etc. And offers excellent support.
entry.summary: Welcome traveller! Are you new to the world of Linux and unsure about which distribution (distro) to choose? With so many options available, it can be quite overwhelming. In this blog post I’ll show you some of the best distros for beginners that cater to various needs and preferences. Either one of these distros I list will leave you well off. Linux Mint Linux mint is one of the most popular Linux distributions among new beginners due to its Windows 10 like interface, and extensive support. It offers a variety of preinstalled applications that cater to various needs such as office productivity, multimedia playback, web browsing, and more. The Linux Mint community provides excellent support through Reddit, forms, and Discord.

entry.title: Welcome to Jekyll!
entry.content:
You’ll find this post in your _posts directory. Go ahead and edit it and re-build the site to see your changes. You can rebuild the site in many different ways, but the most common way is to run jekyll serve, which launches a web server and auto-regenerates your site when a file is updated.

Jekyll requires blog post files to be named according to the following format:

YEAR-MONTH-DAY-title.MARKUP

Where YEAR is a four-digit number, MONTH and DAY are both two-digit numbers, and MARKUP is the file extension representing the format used in the file. After that, include the necessary front matter. Take a look at the source for this post to get an idea about how it works.

Jekyll also offers powerful support for code snippets:

Check out the Jekyll docs for more info on how to get the most out of Jekyll. File all bugs/feature requests at Jekyll’s GitHub repo. If you have questions, you can ask them on Jekyll Talk.
entry.summary: You’ll find this post in your _posts directory. Go ahead and edit it and re-build the site to see your changes. You can rebuild the site in many different ways, but the most common way is to run jekyll serve, which launches a web server and auto-regenerates your site when a file is updated.
